cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Leslie could have gone to the beach. So the problem says that Leslie went to the beach today. We need to find out between what times that could have happened. First, let me list out the information given about Leslie's schedule: - Leslie woke up at 5am. - Emily saw Leslie attending class at school from 5am to 9am. - Anthony saw Leslie watching a movie at the theater from 9am to 11am. - Thomas saw Leslie buying cookies at a bakery from 11am to 4pm. - Ashley saw Leslie fixing their computer at the electronic store from 4pm to 5pm. - Susan saw Leslie walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. - The beach was closed after 10pm. Let me create a timeline of when Leslie was seen doing various activities: 5am - 9am: In class at school (confirmed by Emily) 9am - 11am: Watching a movie at the theater (confirmed by Anthony) 11am - 4pm: Buying cookies at a bakery (confirmed by Thomas) 4pm - 5pm: Fixing computer at electronic store (confirmed by Ashley) 9pm - 10pm: Walking towards Statue of Liberty (confirmed by Susan) The beach was closed after 10pm, so Leslie must have gone to the beach before 10pm. Now, let me look for time gaps when Leslie wasn't seen doing any of these activities. The beach visit must have happened during one of these gaps. From 5am to 9am: In class - not available From 9am to 11am: At theater - not available From 11am to 4pm: At bakery - not available From 4pm to 5pm: At electronic store - not available From 5pm to 9pm: What was Leslie doing? Not mentioned. From 9pm to 10pm: Walking towards Statue of Liberty - not available After 10pm: Beach closed So the only time window when Leslie wasn't seen doing anything is from 5pm to 9pm. This is when Leslie could have gone to the beach. Let me check the options: (A) 5pm to 9pm (B) 9am to 11am (C) 9pm to 10pm (D) 4pm to 5pm Option A is the time when Leslie wasn't seen doing
